game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles II: The Arcade Game

enemy_sprites.name: Foot Soldier (Purple)
enemy_sprites.behavioral_type: color-coded attack pattern, restrain mobility and drain health

enemy_sprites.name: Foot Soldier (Color-variants)
enemy_sprites.behavioral_type: color-coded to indicate attack patterns and weapons

environmental_features: cliffside (planned but absent in Technodrome cutscene)
roadblocks (in level data but hidden behind tires)

distinctive_elements: four playable turtle characters with distinct movesets
color-coded Foot Soldiers indicating attack patterns
special attack animations (jumping attacks, rolling kicks)

player_animations: jump kick (Japanese: any point during jump; Western: descending only)

regional_animation_differences.japanese_version: jump kick can be performed at any point during jump; can push opposite direction after jump kick/special attack to redirect
regional_animation_differences.western_versions: jump kick only during descent; no direction switching after attacks

regional_differences_visual.title_screen_japan: slightly different logo from first NES game, high score display, thicker 'N' in copyright
regional_differences_visual.title_screen_europe: Teenage Mutant Hero Turtles branding instead of Ninja Turtles
regional_differences_visual.difficulty: Japanese version has significantly fewer enemies than Western versions
regional_differences_visual.scoring_system: Japanese version is more robust with different points per enemy type; Western versions give 1 point per enemy
regional_differences_visual.foot_soldier_hp: Japanese: 3 HP (killed by single jump kick); Western: 4 HP (requires 2 jump kicks)

unused_graphics: Pizza Hut logo variant (large, found in dojo level graphics)
smiling face cursor variant (for title/continue screens)
Technodrome cutscene cliffside detail (planned for Scene 5/7)
roadblocks from arcade Scene 3 (hidden in level data behind tires)
